title: cercocarpus montanus var. flabellifolius
intro: Cercocarpus montanus var. flabellifolius, also known as the bush cherry or mountain mahogany, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which are believed to aid in wound healing and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Cercocarpus montanus var. flabellifolius include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its bark, leaves, or roots and are used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
